Job description:



The Opportunity - Join us in shaping a better future for people, communities, and the planet.

Help deliver transformative change that uplifts the performance, capability and impact of Stockland's Finance function. Be part of a team driving improvements that enable better insights, stronger controls and more efficient operations across the enterprise.

We're Looking For

A Finance Transformation Manager who can manage and deliver initiatives that uplift Finance processes, data, systems governance and service delivery for the duration of the project.

What You'll Do

Play a key role in uplifting core Finance processes through simplification and automation, working closely with Finance leaders, Technology, Data, Business Units, and external partners.
Partner effectively with Finance, Technology, the business, and external vendors to deliver agreed outcomes.
Translate strategic priorities into structured, practical delivery plans, managing risks, milestones, documentation, and stakeholder alignment.
Support the development of business cases and translate priorities into executable initiatives with clear outcomes and measurable benefits.
Help drive adoption of new processes, systems, and ways of working across the business.
Build strong, trusted relationships to drive alignment, accountability, and sustainable change.

About You

You are passionate about simplifying and improving Finance processes, and you thrive in environments where analysis, collaboration and structured delivery come together to create meaningful change. You are looking for a role that offers variety, visibility and the opportunity to work closely with multiple stakeholders across the enterprise. This role provides you with the opportunity to have a meaningful impact on the Finance function of a top 50 ASX business.

Experience and Skills That Count

• Strong understanding of core Finance processes including R2R, P2P, O2C and FP and A.
• Experience in transformation, process improvement and knowledge of data governance.
• Strong analytical capability and experience supporting business case development.
• Experience in working with multiple stakeholders.
• Experience with SAP, reconciliation platforms or reporting tools is beneficial.

The Right Mindset

• Collaborative, influential and comfortable working with diverse stakeholder groups.
• Able to navigate ambiguity and bring clarity, structure and momentum.
• Highly organised with strong delivery discipline and attention to detail.

Apply Today

This large-scale transformation is an exciting opportunity to join a high-profile initiative and contribute to its success from the ground up.

The purpose of the Quality Assurance role is to ensure quality management of Program deliverables and outcomes. The role is responsible for maintaining the Program Quality Control and Continuous Improvement Framework (Quality Assurance Plan). The Quality Assurance Analyst will conduct regular Quality Assurance Assessments in line with Program deliverables to ensure they meet agreed outcomes and objectives.

Position Responsibilities

Work with the PMO Managers and Workstream leads in reviewing and assurance quality outcomes for the critical project that align to AEMO and industry standards and agreed Program outcomes in line with the Commonwealth Grant Agreement. This will involve:

Maintaining the Program Quality Control and Continuous Improvement Framework.

Conducting Quality Planning activities such as workshops with Project Leads to ensure alignment with Program objectives.

Support Project Gating activities, including readiness review and compliance checks.

Facilitate Lessons Learnt sessions and maintain Lessons Learnt Register.

Support the Deliverables Management Process and Reporting, as well as performing quality checks on document storage and control process adherence.

Develop monthly quality and performance reporting to report on quality findings, leveraging the quality dashboard and providing commentary on progress and trends.

Generating insights from quality assessment reviews and providing actionable recommendations to senior leaders.

This role will become the Process expert for all project deliverables and will run sessions with the Leadership and Extended leadership, ensuring we have a shared vision and understanding of the Quality Assurance Framework and objectives.

Position Requirements

Skills & Experience:

Proven experience in a Quality Assurance role. - Experience in Australian Energy / Utility industry (preferred)

Experience in project or program quality assurance including quality reviews, compliance, and readiness assessments across complex programs

Strong understanding of governance and associated management frameworks

High proficiency with Microsoft tools and records management systems.

Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills, able to guide team towards planned quality outcomes

Ability to build trusted relationships with the project managers, PMO, and governance bodies

High attention to detail, demonstrate critical thinking and problem-solving skills

Strong risk awareness, able to identify emerging issues and escalate appropriately.

Must be an Australian citizen with the ability to attain and maintain an appropriate level of national security clearance.

Education/ professional Certifications: 

Tertiary and/or post graduate qualifications in Business Administration, or related field.

Professional certifications in Project Management preferred.

In return some of our benefits to you

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more.

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:


Help shape the future of Australia's largest UKG practice
Join a national community of 90+ UKG specialists within Deloitte's Human Capital practice
Work on complex workforce transformation programs with some of Australia's most recognised organisations

About the Team

Deloitte's UKG practice is the largest in Australia and continues to grow rapidly. Our team brings together specialists who design and deliver workforce technology solutions that help organisations better manage, schedule and support their people.

Joining Deloitte means working on large-scale UKG implementations that transform how organisations operate. Our teams deliver across industries including health, retail, supply chain, hospitality and the public sector, solving complex workforce challenges and enabling organisations to operate more effectively.

You'll work across the full UKG ecosystem, supported by specialists in timekeeping, advanced scheduling, forecasting, analytics, reporting and integrations.

What sets this team apart is the depth of expertise around you. With more than 90 UKG specialists across Australia, and access to Deloitte's global UKG community supporting hundreds of clients worldwide, you'll join a genuine community of practice where collaboration, knowledge sharing and continuous learning are central to how we work.

What will your typical day look like?

As a Specialist Lead within our Workforce Payroll Solutions team, you will play a key role delivering UKG Pro Workforce Management (WFM) implementation programs for leading organisations.

You'll combine deep product expertise with consulting capability to design and deliver workforce technology solutions that integrate seamlessly within a client's broader HR and payroll technology landscape.

Your responsibilities will include:

Designing and architecting UKG Pro WFM solutions aligned to client business needs
Facilitating client workshops to capture and translate workforce management requirements
Advising on best use of UKG functionality, integrations and user experience
Configuring and supporting the implementation of UKG WFM modules
Guiding clients through complex workforce design decisions
Collaborating with project teams to deliver solutions aligned to project timelines
Mentoring team members and contributing to capability development across the UKG practice
Staying up to date with the latest UKG releases, innovations and certifications

Our teams typically work across one to three projects at a time, allowing you to focus on delivering meaningful outcomes and high-quality solutions for clients.

Purpose & Impact

Our work combines technology, business insight and practical delivery to help organisations transform how they manage and support their workforce.

In this role, you will:

Deliver complex UKG implementation programs that improve workforce operations and employee experience
Work with leading organisations across health, retail, supply chain, hospitality and government
Contribute to end-to-end workforce transformation alongside specialists in payroll, HCM, workforce integrity, organisational design and change management
Help shape the future of Australia's largest UKG practice

People & Culture

You'll be part of a national community of more than 90 UKG specialists who collaborate openly and support each other's success.

Our team brings together functional consultants, developers and transformation specialists, creating an environment where people learn from each other and solve complex problems together.

You can expect:

A strong knowledge-sharing culture supported by communities of practice
Regular learning sessions and team connection events
Collaboration across the broader Deloitte Human Capital practice

Learning & Growth

This role offers the opportunity to build deep expertise in workforce technology while developing a broader consulting career. Deloitte invests heavily in capability development, innovation and knowledge sharing across the practice.

You will:

Learn from some of Australia's most experienced UKG specialists
Build expertise across the full UKG ecosystem including integrations, reporting and DataHub
Gain experience across multiple industries and large-scale transformation programs
Contribute to innovation initiatives including accelerators and AI-enabled workforce solutions

Enough about us — let's talk about you

You enjoy working at the intersection of workforce operations, technology and consulting.

You bring strong expertise in UKG Pro Workforce Management and enjoy partnering with clients to solve complex workforce challenges and deliver practical, scalable solutions.

What you'll bring

Proven experience working with UKG Pro Workforce Management (WFM) as a lead consultant or practitioner.
Experience translating business requirements into functional workforce technology solutions
Specialist knowledge in one or more WFM areas such as:
Time & Attendance
Optimised Rostering
Forecasting
Activities
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Demonstrated problem-solving and decision-making capability
Ability to manage multiple priorities across client delivery projects
Strong attention to detail and commitment to high-quality delivery
Tertiary qualifications in Business, HR, IT or related disciplines (advantageous)

Why Deloitte?  

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and our society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.   
 We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.  
We value in-person connection with our clients and our colleagues. We offer several ways for you to work flexibly so that you can serve your clients, stay connected with your team, and manage your personal priorities.
We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.

About us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation. As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world's most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.




About the program:

AIR6500 is the RAAF’s $3 billion program to develop a joint air battle management system that builds on the capabilities offered by Next Generation sensor and command and control systems. AIR6500 will create a fully networked ‘system of systems’ that synchronises air and missile defence operations in joint and coalition environments, maximising situational awareness and enhancing the speed of decision making across the battlespace.





About the role:

We are seeking an experienced Logistics Support Analysis Lead to join the AIR6500 team, supporting the delivery of Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) and Logistics Support Analysis (LSA) activities within an Agile program environment.

In this role, you will work closely with the ILS Lead and Systems Engineering, Integration and Test (SEIT) Team, to:

Plan and lead the team to execute Logistics Support Analysis (LSA) work packages within the Program’s Agile framework.

Develop technical LSA studies and evaluations of engineering design concepts.

Lead and Mentor the team tasked to capture LSA data in associated templates and databases.

Develop and compile plans, studies and reports as required contractually.

Lead workshops with internal and external stakeholders to define and tailor the LSA program.

Ensure compliance with standards, certification and assurance to support system design certification.

Serve as SME on LSA activities, providing advice and guidance to internal and external stakeholders.

 

About you:

You are an experienced LSA professional who has a deep knowledge of stakeholder management and ability to identify, analyse, select, document and design for all LSA requirements. You bring:

Deep knowledge of ILS process, procedures and the overall engineering product lifecycle.

Defence Industry Project delivery experience including, specialist technical application of Integrated Logistics Support including Logistics Support Analysis (LSA) tasks, sparing, maintenance support, training and documentation to engineering projects.

Extensive experience with supportability analysis, MRD, and Repairable Item Management.

Leadership of large teams to deliver successful outcomes.

Effective technical guidance, mentoring and training to other employees.

Deep understanding of working with and leading contractual processes and deliverables.


Desirable:

Knowledge of adjacent ILS disciplines including training and publications.

Experience using tools such as MILIS/ERP, CAMM2, OPUS10, Eagle or similar logistics/reliability systems.

Experience in Agile development application.


Why join us:

Contribute to a nationally critical Joint Air Battle Management capability

Work within a complex, multi-domain Defence system-of-systems program

Collaborate with Australian and US teams on advanced Defence technologies




How we reward and support you: 

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

Flexible work & Hybrid work options

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Annual Flu Vaccinations, Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs




Special Requirements:

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

Veterans are encouraged to apply. We highly value the unique skills and experiences that veterans and transitioning service members can contribute to our team. We are proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Australian Government.

We are looking for a Senior Evaluation Consultant to join our growing management consultancy in Melbourne.

Do you want your expertise to be in the room where decisions get made, and to make a genuine difference to people's lives and wellbeing?

Have you spent time honing your evaluation craft and are ready to use it in complex, fast-moving, high-impact consulting?

If this is you, Allen + Clarke wants to hear from you.

About us

Allen + Clarke is a values-driven management consultancy specialising in evaluation, public policy, strategy, and organisational development. We are trusted advisors to government, non-government and mission-driven organisations across Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific. We have offices in Melbourne and Wellington.

Our company vision is to empower clients and communities to overcome the challenges facing society.

We give a damn. That means we believe in being and giving our best, the importance of people and always being authentic.

Our consultants are flexible, hard-working and committed. They care and want to make a difference by delivering excellent work for our clients. They know how to work at pace. They never lose sight of quality. They are good at identifying opportunities, risks and issues and mitigating them to ensure success for our clients. They enjoy looking for business development opportunities to make our impact sustainable.

The opportunity

This is a senior role for an experienced evaluator. We are seeking a consultant with 10 years or more professional experience delivering evaluation services.

As a Senior Evaluation Consultant, you'll design and deliver evaluations across health, human services, justice, education, and broader social sector programs and policies. You will work with Commonwealth and state government clients, NGOs, and private sector organisations. You will help clients understand what's working, what isn't, and what to do about it — and you'll do it in a way that's practical, credible, and useful.

Our evaluation work is designed to make a difference. We choose the right approach for the question, stay focused on what clients need to know, and deliver pragmatic insights they can use.

As you grow with us, you will have the opportunity to take on greater responsibility shaping our approach to evaluation practice and contributing to the strategic direction of the consultancy. You will have a key role how we develop and innovate our evaluation offering, and we will create space for you to do that.

In a typical week, you might:

Meet a client to understand their context and identify how our evaluation expertise can help them

Lead Project Teams across several evaluation projects for a range of clients with varying outputs, including for example a mixed-methods, multi-site evaluation; an Outcomes Framework and a Monitoring and Evaluation Framework.

Lead a team brainstorm to design an evaluation approach and shape our value proposition for a proposal

Design data collection instruments such as interview guides, surveys, or focus group protocols that are tailored to the evaluation questions and participants

Conduct interviews or stakeholder engagement sessions as part of primary data collection

Analyse qualitative and/or quantitative data and pull out what it's telling us

Write up evaluation results into a compelling narrative, translating evidence into clear, actionable findings and recommendations

Facilitate a participatory interpretation session to help a client make sense of emerging findings

Peer review a colleague's report and support them to lift the quality of the analysis

Check in on your projects, tracking time, budget, and risks to ensure everything is on track.

About you

You are a values-driven person, keen to bring about meaningful social change. You are a strategic thinker with strong analytical skills and the ability to translate complex evidence into practical recommendations.

Key Selection Criteria:

We want to hear from you if you bring:

10 years plus experience conducting evaluations, ideally in a consultancy setting

The ability to lead complex evaluations end-to-end, including design, delivery, reporting, and dissemination

Strong skills in qualitative and/or quantitative methods, and the ability to design the right approach to answer the evaluation questions

Knowledge of evaluation theories, frameworks, and methodologies, and the judgement to apply them pragmatically

Proven ability to manage project teams, mentor less experienced colleagues, and maintain quality across multiple workstreams

Confidence in engaging openly and flexibly with clients and building their trust

A commercial mindset: you understand the value of time, you're motivated to deliver efficiently, and you know how to write a compelling proposal

Excellent communication skills, with the ability to develop clear, compelling reports, briefings, and presentations that cut through complexity

Cultural competence and the ability to engage with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and other diverse communities in a culturally safe and respectful way

Familiarity with, or active interest in, how AI tools can improve the efficiency and quality of evaluation work, alongside the critical thinking to use them appropriately and ethically

The ability to manage multiple priorities and consistently deliver high-quality work to deadlines.

Key personal attributes:

have a can-do attitude, are highly organised and can work independently

have a strong client focus.

 What we offer

This is an ongoing role based in Melbourne and may require some domestic and international travel. We offer flexible working arrangements with the expectation that the majority of the work week is spent in the office (that means three days a week for full-time staff).

We will provide a supportive, collaborative and collegial work environment with opportunities to mentor less experienced team members and to develop your evaluation consultancy skills alongside other experienced practitioners.

We are committed to providing a supportive and culturally safe working environment for all our staff members, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander employees.

Given the nature of our work, applicants must be Australian citizens (eligible for Australian Government security clearance).

You can find out more about us at www.allenandclarke.com.au   

How to apply

Applications close 5pm Thursday 9 April 2026.

Applicants should provide:

a cover letter addressing the key selection criteria outlined above, noting that it is not expected that applicants will meet all the Key Selection Criteria outlined

a succinct curriculum vitae or resume of no more than 4 pages.

Applicants may apply via Seek or Ethical Jobs platforms.

Applicants must confirm they are eligible for Australian government security clearance, which is limited to Australian citizens.

If you have any questions, please contact recruitmentau@allenandclarke.com.

Manager - Business Consulting

The opportunity

As a Manager in the Finance Consulting team, you will play a pivotal role in helping clients navigate complex, time‑critical finance challenges and own delivery end‑to‑end, from mobilisation through to final recommendations and outcomes.

You will lead multi‑workstream finance engagements, partnering closely with CFOs and senior finance leaders to provide clear judgement, commercial insight, and pragmatic solutions. Your work will span strategic, operational, and digital finance transformation, including the design and implementation of future‑ready finance operating models, enterprise performance improvement, and technology‑enabled transformation, including the integration of Agentic AI into Finance functions.

This role is based in Warrang (Sydney) and offers the opportunity to sit at the centre of delivery, decision‑making, and people leadership in one of the world's leading professional services firms.

Your key responsibilities

Lead complex finance transformation projects involving digital strategy, process optimisation, future-state operating model design, and the implementation of enabling technologies such as ERP (SAP, Microsoft Dynamics etc), EPM, AI, RPA, and Power BI. Engage directly with CFOs, GMs, and senior finance leaders, leading senior‑level conversations, framing decisions, and clearly articulating implications, trade‑offs, and the "so what".
Inspire and guide high-performing teams by mentoring junior consultants, collaborating across service lines, and delivering integrated solutions that drive sustainable change.
Provide strategic insights, and support business development through proposal leadership, solution design, and thought leadership, with a strong focus on commercial judgement and delivery credibility.

Skills and attributes for success

4-8 years of experience in finance transformation, consulting, or in-house finance roles, ideally within professional services, Big 4, or industry finance teams.
Strong knowledge of core finance functions (e.g. FP&A, record-to-report, order-to-cash), with hands-on experience in finance strategy, process improvement, and system implementations.
Proven ability to lead and influence with strong communication, stakeholder management, and problem-solving skills; experience with tools like SAP, Microsoft, Blackline, or Tagetik is a plus. One of the sectors we work with is the Australian Federal Government and as such Australian Citizenship is preferred.

Ideally, you'll also have the skills and attributes below but don't worry if you don't tick all the boxes. We're interested in your aptitude, attitude and willingness to learn.

Relevant qualifications such as CA, CPA, CFA, or MBA
A finance related degree

The Tender Specialist role is a standalone, strategic role responsible for the end-to-end delivery of high-quality, competitive tenders that support business growth and client retention. The role combines bid ownership, strategic content development, and senior stakeholder engagement, with a strong focus on leveraging AI-enabled tender response technologies to improve quality, consistency, and win rates.
Operating with a high degree of autonomy, you will partner directly with senior leaders and subject matter experts across the organisation to shape bid strategy, curate compelling responses, and continuously improve tender capability.

Key Responsibilities

1. End-to-End Tender Ownership

Own and manage the full tender lifecycle for mid-sized opportunities, from receipt and strategy through to submission and post-bid review.
Interpret tender documentation, evaluation criteria, and commercial requirements to develop clear, compliant response strategies.
Act as the central point of coordination for all tender-related activity.
2. Strategic, AI-Enabled Proposal Development

Leverage internally developed AI-powered tender response technology to source, adapt, and refine high-performing content.
Apply professional judgement to edit, tailor, and validate AI-assisted responses, ensuring accuracy, relevance, tone, and compliance.
Continuously strengthen the AI content database by capturing successful responses, insights, and lessons learned.
3. Senior Stakeholder Engagement

Work directly with senior leaders and subject matter experts to gather inputs and approvals.
Challenge and guide stakeholders on positioning, differentiators, and messaging to strengthen bid quality.
Manage review processes and approvals independently.
4. Pitch & Presentation Development

Develop strategic, client-ready pitch materials that clearly articulate value, capability, and competitive advantage.
Ensure consistency of narrative across written proposals and presentations.
Collaborate with Marketing to ensure submissions meet brand standards, utilise approved templates, and incorporate high‑quality design elements.
5. Content Strategy & Continuous Improvement

Own and evolve all tender content, case studies, and standard responses within AI-enabled platforms.
Analyse tender outcomes and feedback to identify trends, gaps, and opportunities for improvement.
Drive continuous improvement in bid quality, efficiency, and effectiveness.
6. Governance & Delivery

Manage multiple tenders concurrently, prioritising workloads and meeting tight deadlines.
Ensure all submissions comply with brand, governance, risk, and regulatory requirements.
Essential skills and experience

Minimum 3 years’ experience in bid management, tendering, or a related field, including strong end-to-end tender ownership.
Proven experience working autonomously and managing complex bids without a dedicated bid team.
Experience using content management systems and/or AI-enabled tools to support proposal development.
Desirable experience in working with government tenders.
Strong strategic, analytical, and editorial capability.
Excellent project management skills and ability to manage competing priorities.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Word and PowerPoint.
Strong stakeholder management skills, including influencing senior leaders.
Highly self-directed, accountable, and resilient.
Strategic thinker with strong attention to detail.
Confident working with emerging technologies, including AI.
Collaborative, pragmatic, and outcomes focused.
Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.
Prior experience within the insurance or finance industry highly desirable.

Our commitment to Diversity, Equity & Inclusion

Finity is an equal opportunity employer. At Finity, we believe that Diversity and Inclusion are fundamental to who we are, our core values and how we operate. We believe in, and value, a diverse workforce where differences are respected and diversity of thought, experiences, skills, backgrounds and beliefs are brought together in a respectful and inclusive environment.

Guiding the future of NSW education 

About the role


The Lead, Measurement and Research Services is a senior quantitative leadership role within NESA’s Assessment Division, responsible for supporting NSW-wide assessment programs, including the Higher School Certificate (HSC), the HSC Minimum Standards Tests, and NAPLAN.

The role involves computational modelling to translate policy and assessment frameworks into precise mathematical and statistical formulations, supported by intensive analytical coding in R. It requires strong quantitative judgement to design, implement, and evaluate models that underpin high-stakes decisions, often under tight time constraints.

You will work across a wide range of analytical problems, including complex modelling, large-scale data analysis, model evaluation, and analytical quality assurance to ensure outputs are robust, defensible, and fit for purpose. In addition to hands-on technical work, the role provides leadership and guidance to a team of statisticians and data scientists, promoting high standards of analytical practice, reproducibility, and documentation.

A key component of the role is advising and communicating with senior internal stakeholders, governance bodies, and expert committees. You will present and explain complex quantitative findings to non-technical audiences, support policy deliberations, and contribute to decision-making processes where analytical rigour and clarity are critical.

This role offers an opportunity to apply deep quantitative problem-solving skills to work with real-world impact, influencing outcomes for students across NSW.

Why join NESA

Just some of the benefits of working with NESA include:

An organisation where your work has a meaningful impact on HSC and NAPLAN outcomes, supporting equity and excellence in education.
A prime CBD location (all modes of transport nearby, and excellent coffee and eateries) with refurbished offices.
Flexible working arrangements and generous leave entitlements with standard working hours 35 hours per week.
Accrue flex time for hours worked over the standard hours and enjoy true work-life balance.
A range of leave to support you with your needs out of work including generous paid parental leave, carers’ leave and more.
Access to discounted health and fitness memberships via Fitness Passport, an employee assistance program and annual flu vaccinations.
Salary packaging options.
Working in a purpose driven and ethical organisation with committed colleagues.

 

Position details
Clerk Grade 11/12
Full-Time, Ongoing
Close to Wynyard station & hybrid work arrangements available 

About us


At the NSW Education Standards Authority (NESA), we are dedicated to carrying out meaningful work that drives improvements and elevates student achievement across NSW, now and into the future.

We accomplish this by supporting all school sectors with high-quality syllabuses, assessment (including managing the HSC and NAPLAN), teaching standards (e.g., accrediting teachers) and school environments (including setting and monitoring school standards).

NESA is a unique organisation in NSW (of around 740 FTE staff) with significant state-wide impact, visit our website to learn more about the important work we do.


On a day-to-day basis you will be responsible for: 
Leading the unit in delivering core functions, particularly during the HSC written examination period.
Solving complex and time-critical quantitative problems, including diagnosing and validating analytical logic in complex systems with limited documentation.
Designing and implementing robust and automated analytical workflows that generate high-stakes quantitative outputs for ministerial, executive, and statutory reporting.
Proactively identifying and responding to emerging issues to ensure effective resolution and minimise delivery and analytical risks.
Leading and contributing to projects that support strategic and operational objectives.
Providing expert quantitative advice to senior stakeholders, governance committees, and policy forums, including clear communication of complex technical issues to non-technical audiences.
Leading, mentoring, and providing technical oversight to statisticians and data scientists, including code and modelling review and capability development.
Exercising professional judgement in determining appropriate analytical approaches, modelling assumptions, and trade-offs in time-critical contexts to ensure outputs are accurate, defensible, and fit for purpose.

 

To be successful you will need:
PhD in Mathematics, Data Science, Statistics, Engineering, Physics, Computer Science or other quantitative science or equivalent professional experience demonstrating comparable depth in large-scale, high-stakes quantitative modelling and analysis.
Strong analytical thinking, problem‑solving ability and attention to detail.
Experience in data wrangling, scientific computing, modelling and visualising using R (primary) and Python.
Familiarity with version control systems (Git) and reproducible coding standards and ensuring consistent adoption of these practices across direct reports.
High-level written, verbal, and presentation skills, with the ability to communicate clearly and engage internal and external stakeholders.
Demonstrated experience preparing high-quality technical documentation using LaTeX, RMarkdown, or Quarto.
Willingness to work outside standard working hours during the HSC period (paid), with the ability to remain focused and work efficiently under high pressure. 
Demonstrated ability to lead teams in delivering fit-for-purpose, high-quality outcomes within business timeframes, making informed trade-offs where required.
Demonstrated commitment to cultivating a team culture that embraces ongoing learning in emerging technologies and modelling techniques.

 

It is desirable to have:
Experience in psychometric modelling, including knowledge of Item Response Theory.
Proficiency in TAM, dplyr and SQL.
Familiarity with Generative AI and AI-assisted or agentic coding tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot, Codex, Cursor), with the judgement to support their safe and appropriate use within the team.
Experience in leading and coaching data scientists and statisticians in a fast-paced environment, applying advanced statistical and, where appropriate, machine learning techniques to complex datasets. 
Experience using cloud-based platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud) and Linux environments to support scientific computing or large-scale analytical workloads.

Job Title: Solutions Engineer / Pre Sales Consultant (Risk & Audit)New
Ad Text:

This role is ideal for someone from a Risk, Audit, or Compliance background who enjoys working closely with customers, solving complex problems, and presenting thoughtful, high-impact solutions in a commercial environment.

As a Solutions Engineer at Diligent, you will partner closely with Sales to help organisations understand how our platform can transform the way they manage risk, audit, and compliance. Key Responsibilities:

Customer & Commercial Partnership


Partner closely with Sales to support the full pre-sales lifecycle, helping customers understand how Diligent solves real-world risk and audit challenges.

Deliver tailored, high-impact software demonstrations that clearly articulate business value not just product features.

Engage confidently with senior leaders across Audit, Risk, Compliance, IT, and Governance functions.

Translate customer pain points into clear solution narratives that support deal progression and closure.

Solution Design & Thought Leadership


Design and present solution architectures that align with customer objectives and regulatory environments.

Articulate the cross-platform value of the Diligent Platform and how it supports integrated GRC programs.

Collaborate with Product teams to provide customer and domain feedback on product enhancements.

Domain Expertise

Leverage familiarity with industry frameworks such as COSO ERM, ISO 31000, NIST RMF, and related standards.

Demonstrate how modern GRC platforms enable risk identification, assessment, monitoring, and mitigation at scale.

Experience working with SaaS solutions or strong interest in moving into a commercial, customer-facing environment.

Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics.

Comfortable partnering with Sales and contributing to commercial outcomes.

Curious, self-driven, and motivated to continuously deepen both technical and domain knowledge.

#LI-SM1

#LI-Hybrid

At Diligent, we're building the future with people who think boldly and move fast.  Whether you're designing systems that leverage large language models or part of a team reimaging workflows with AI, you'll help us unlock entirely new ways of working and thinking.  Curiosity is in our DNA, we look for individuals willing to ask the big questions and experiment fearlessly - those who embrace change not as a challenge, but as an opportunity.  The future belongs to those who keep learning, and we are building it together.  At Diligent, you’re not just building the future - you’re an agent of positive change, joining a global community on a mission to make an impact.

Smartways is scaling quickly, and the complexity of our operation is increasing. Without this role, we add more people to solve problems. With this role, we design the problems out of the system.

At Smartways, Customer Operations is the safety net of our business — ensuring critical healthcare deliveries reach patients, hospitals and clinics exactly when they need to.

We are now looking for someone to take that safety net and make it smarter, faster and more scalable. This is not a reporting role. This is not a traditional workforce planning role.

This is a role for someone who can see how an operation really runs — and redesign it using data, automation and AI.

What you will own

You will sit within our leadership team and take ownership of how our operation is measured, optimised and scaled.

1.   Build Clarity through data

·       Own and evolve our Power BI and reporting environment

·       Bring together multiple systems into a single, trusted view of performance

·       Identify what’s actually driving workload, inefficiency and cost

·       Turn data into decisions — not dashboards

2.   Redesign how the operation works

·       Identify and eliminate unmeasured or unnecessary workload

·       Improve how work is routed, prioritised and completed

·       Build scalable frameworks that reduce reliance on manual effort

·       Model the financial and operational impact of change

3. Lead automation & AI adoption

·       Identify opportunities to automate repetitive or low-value work

·       Design and implement solutions utilising HubSpot, Dialpad and internal systems

·       Introduce AI to improve: Forecasting accuracy, Customer interactions and Task allocation

·       Drive real, measurable ROI from automation initiatives

4. Enable smarter workforce planning

·       Develop forecasts across calls, digital channels and back-office work

·       Design schedules that balance service, cost and team experience

·       Model future resource requirements based on growth and change

·       Improve how we respond to intraday performance challenges

What we’re looking for

We’re not looking for a traditional planner or a passive analyst. We’re looking for someone who:

·       Thinks like an operator, not just an analyst

·       Can move from insight → action → implementation

·       Is comfortable working across systems, data and people

·       Has a strong bias for simplifying and automating

What you will bring

·       Strong analytics capability (Power BI, SQL, Excel or similar)

·       Experience improving or redesigning operational processes

·       Exposure to automation, AI tools or workflow design

·       Experience in contact centres, logistics or service operations (preferred)

·       Workforce planning experience is valuable — but not the core of the role

Why join Smartways

·       Access to the Executive team and real decision-making influence

·       Ownership of high-impact transformation initiatives

·       The opportunity to shape how AI is actually used in operations

·       A role where your work directly improves outcomes for patients and healthcare providers

Ad Text:
About the role

We are seeking an experienced and dynamic Manager who is driven, focused and passionate about shaping the future of healthcare infrastructure.

As the Manager, Reporting & Analysis you will be responsible for leading and managing an effective team in providing a state-wide analysis and reporting function on financial and non-financial information for capital projects and programs across Queensland Health.

The role provides leadership, guidance, coaching, analysis and advice to Senior Management, the broader Department, and Hospital and Health Services on a broad range of complex, sensitive and time-critical reporting projects. You will have direct reports and will be required to promote an effective team environment that produces quality timely outcomes. 

Key Responsibilities

Provide expert level advice technical leadership on reporting, related projects and issues
Develop and maintain an effective reporting process and system to the delivery of capital projects and programs
Manage the provision of accurate and timely reporting information for government budget cycle requirements

About You

Demonstrated experience leading high-functioning teams within a complex environment.
Strong skills in strategic, conceptional and critical thinking
The ability to analyse a range of financial controls
Experience with data collection and reporting systems, business intelligence tools and programming languages (such as SAP S/4 HANA, Power BI/DAX and SQL).

How to Apply

To apply please submit the following information:

Your current resume, including two referees who have a thorough knowledge of your capabilities, work performance and conduct within the previous two years. It is preferable to include your current, immediate, or past supervisor.
A cover letter (maximum 1-2 pages, dot points acceptable) including the below:
An outline of how your experience, abilities, knowledge and personal qualities would enable you to achieve the key responsibilities and meet the skill requirements of the role.
Responses to the following questions:
Question 1 – What is your motivation to join Health Infrastructure Queensland?
Question 2 – Outline which HIQ commitment you align the most closely with and provide an example of when you have lived this commitment.

Countless opportunities to learn and ongoing professional development.
